findings.issue: Round-1 major finding partially resolved. 'Критерии приёмки' and 'Технические решения' were rewritten to the behavior / decision-rationale level as requested (no more manifest-attribute enumeration in AC, no more ActivityResultContracts / Process.killProcess API calls in AC, and 'Технические решения' now reads as trade-off decisions). However, two pockets of implementation leakage remain: (a) 'Тестирование' still lists concrete class/method names (LogExportManager.composeExport(), DeviceInfoCollector.collect(), CrashLogWriter.write(Throwable), LogcatReader.read(pid), CrashState.shouldShowBanner()) as unit-test targets — these were explicitly called out in the round-1 fix; (b) 'Как должно работать' narrative (esp. US-A step 2, US-B, dev-gesture) still embeds low-level specifics (Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler, Process.killProcess, android:process=":crash", ACTION_CREATE_DOCUMENT + MIME text/plain, logcat -d -v threadtime --pid=<ownpid> *:E, filesDir/logs/crash.log.dismissed, RuntimeException("test crash from About")).
findings.why_matters: 'Тестирование' prescribing exact method signatures pins the tech-spec author to a specific decomposition before TS is even written — it also makes the user-spec churn whenever the implementer chooses different names (e.g. merging CrashLogWriter into CrashHandler). 'Как должно работать' at API level mixes product intent with implementation, making the spec harder to read at the 'product manager' level and preempting tech-spec decisions. This is no longer blocking — the AC cleanup moved the core product contract to behavior level, and Тестирование class names act more as 'decomposition hints' than hard requirements. Downgrading from major to minor.
findings.fix: Optional — can be handled during tech-spec drafting: (1) Rewrite 'Тестирование' bullets at behavior level — e.g. 'a header/format composer is unit-tested for: section order, empty/unavailable placeholders, logcat truncation to the documented cap, crash-log truncation to the documented cap' instead of naming LogExportManager.composeExport(). Let tech-spec pick the class names. (2) Strip API calls from 'Как должно работать' narrative, keeping only user-visible behavior (e.g. 'an uncaught exception in the main process causes the app to write a crash record to disk synchronously, then show a crash-report screen in a separate process that survives main-process death' instead of naming Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler and android:process). If the user prefers to lock these details in user-spec for continuity with the phase-1/phase-2 style, document that as a deliberate deviation in tech-spec.

findings.issue: Round-1 minor finding on missing edge cases is fully resolved. All four requested cases landed in AC: (1) IOException incl. no-space → line 65; (2) 0-byte crash.log via the 'Отсутствующий или пустой' + 'тогда и только тогда, когда запись о краше есть на диске' combination → lines 71 + 87; (3) fresh-install empty filesDir/logs/ → line 93 (new 'Инвариант на установке и в пустом состоянии' section); (4) double-tap SAF → line 67.
findings.why_matters: Noted for traceability only — no further action needed.

findings.issue: Round-1 minor finding on ACRA acknowledgement is resolved. Line 119 now explicitly records: 'ACRA рассмотрена ... она под Apache-2.0 совместима с манифестом, но для нашего объёма (~200 строк кода, нет server-side бэкенда, нет конфигурации) это внешняя зависимость ради чужой схемы, а не наш выбор.' — the question is now settled in the spec and cannot be re-opened in review.

summary: Round-1 fixes verified. Major scope-leak was substantially addressed (AC and Технические решения now describe behavior and decisions rather than APIs and manifest attributes); residual implementation-detail leakage in 'Тестирование' and narrative sections remains but is downgraded to minor. Edge cases added. ACRA explicitly rejected with rationale. :crash split decision locked by user — documented as known non-blocker. No new adequacy or feasibility issues introduced. Approved.
